Main Objectives: 1. Improve building condition.; 2. Decrease energy use.; 3. Develop a thorough and complete project plan for retrofits.; 4. Showcase project objectives and results to the community.
Key Actions: 1. Survey buildings (layout, insulation, system condition, safety).; 2. Conduct building energy audits using AkWarm-C software.; 3. Develop an Energy Action Plan (draft, present, revise, finalize).; 4. Develop a data monitoring plan.; 5. Collect baseline data (energy use, condition, comfort).; 6. Create a monthly maintenance checklist.; 7. Identify financing options for retrofits.; 8. Conduct outreach activities.
Stakeholders: • Cold Climate Housing Research Center (CCHRC); • Energy Audits of Alaska; • Aniak Traditional Council / The Tribe; • Building staff / occupants; • Tribal finance director; • Tribal maintenance manager; • Community members

Main Objectives: Showcase the objective and results of the project to community members, Alaskans, and others.
Key Actions: Conduct outreach activities; store project documents (flyers, scope of work, audit summaries) in appendices.

Location: Tribal office file cabinet; CCHRC server/website
Main Objectives: Store project data for reference and future use.
Key Actions: Store hard copies of fuel/electrical data; store building condition/occupant comfort data (if collected); store baseline data on CCHRC server and website.
Stakeholders: The Tribe, Tribal administrator, Cold Climate Housing Research Center (CCHRC)

Main Objectives: Perform regular building maintenance and inspections.
Key Actions: **Police Station:** Check/configure thermostat; create heating parts list; ensure lighting is operational/LED; check office equipment plug loads; inspect exterior for damage; check smoke/CO detectors. **ATC Shop:** Monthly visual exterior inspection; document damage.
Stakeholders: Police station staff, ATC shop staff

Main Objectives: Develop a strong project plan for funding applications.
Key Actions: Use audit info to define scope, finances, timeline, personnel; identify risks/mitigation strategies; reach out to other communities for advice.

Main Objectives: Plan and initiate the energy audit project.
Key Actions: Hold kickoff meeting to decide metrics; develop data monitoring plan (collection, storage, access, analysis); collect baseline data (energy use, occupant comfort).
Stakeholders: Council, Project staff, Tribal office staff, Building occupants

Main Objectives: Address key questions for project planning.
Key Actions: Identify local retrofit contractors; determine retrofit financing options; obtain historical energy use data (2016, 2017); decide on building monitoring methods (bills, surveys, devices).
